title: Te Arotakenga Hōtaka Hangarau mō te Taiao | Technology Impact Assessment for Our Environment
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will investigate the environmental impact of technology through the lens of Ngāti Kahungunu kaitiakitanga, focusing specifically on the Mangakōtukutuku Stream restoration project. They will explore how traditional Māori environmental knowledge (mātauranga Māori) can inform modern technological solutions to improve water quality and ecosystem health.
learningObjectives: Analyze water quality data from Mangakōtukutuku Stream using digital monitoring tools and identify key environmental concerns
Design a technological solution that integrates mātauranga Māori principles with modern engineering to address a specific stream restoration challenge
Create a digital presentation that effectively communicates findings and proposed solutions to community stakeholders using both te reo Māori and English
Evaluate the potential environmental and cultural impacts of proposed technological solutions using a sustainability framework

activities.title: Whakawhanaungatanga ki te Awa | Connecting to the Stream
activities.description: Visit Mangakōtukutuku Stream with local kaumātua to learn the pūrākau (traditional stories) of the waterway and conduct initial water quality testing using digital pH meters, turbidity tubes, and temperature sensors

activities.title: He Tātari Raraunga | Data Analysis Workshop
activities.description: Analyze collected water quality data using spreadsheet software, comparing results against regional council standards and historical data to identify trends and concerning patterns

activities.title: He Whakaaro Hangarau | Technology Design Challenge
activities.description: In small groups, design a technological solution (e.g., automated planting drone, smart sediment trap, digital monitoring system) that addresses an identified stream issue while honoring Māori environmental principles

activities.title: He Kōrerorero ki te Hapori | Community Consultation
activities.description: Present prototype designs to local Ngāti Kahungunu representatives and Hawke's Bay Regional Council staff for feedback, incorporating community perspectives into final designs

activities.title: He Whakatairanga i te Kaupapa | Solution Showcase
activities.description: Create digital presentations (using tools like Canva or Google Slides) that showcase final designs, including technical specifications, cultural considerations, and implementation plans in both English and te reo Māori

resources: Hawke's Bay Regional Council water quality monitoring data portal and testing equipment
Local kaumātua and environmental kaitiaki from Ngāti Kahungunu for cultural guidance
Digital design software (Tinkercad, SketchUp) and prototyping materials
Māori Environmental Knowledge: Concepts and Perspectives by Merata Kawharu (reference text)

tasks: Water quality data analysis report with identified concerns
Technical design blueprint of proposed solution
Cultural impact assessment of the technology

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice: Planning for practice, brief development, outcome development and evaluation
Nature of Technology: Characteristics of technology, characteristics of technological outcomes
Technological Knowledge: Technological modelling, technological products
Key Competencies: Thinking, using language symbols and texts, participating and contributing
